result.stdout:
Description:
  RabbitMQ topology infrastructure-as-code for CI/CD and operator automation.
  
  Commands:
    validate  Validate topology files without contacting RabbitMQ.
    init      Create a topology YAML template.
    plan      Compare desired topology with the broker. Never changes broker state.
    apply     Apply only safe reconciliation operations. Refuses destructive changes.
    purge     Remove messages from all queues declared by the topology. Requires confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
    destroy   Intentionally delete declared virtual hosts. Requires confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
    export    Export broker topology to YAML.
    completion  Print shell completion scripts.
  
  Examples:
    sprmq init --template minimal --output-file topology.yaml
    sprmq plan --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml
    sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq purge --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --allow-destructive --auto-approve
    sprmq completion zsh

Usage:
  sprmq [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  init                Create a topology YAML file from a built-in template.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq init --template retry-dead-letter --output-file topology.yaml
  validate            Validate topology YAML syntax, normalization, and semantic consistency.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq validate --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml
  plan                Compare desired topology against broker state and print a reconciliation plan.
                      This command never changes broker state.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq plan --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml
  apply               Apply a topology plan to RabbitMQ.
                      This command only performs safe reconciliation and refuses destructive plans unless --migrate is provided.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
                        sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --migrate
  purge               Remove all messages from every queue declared by the normalized topology.
                      Generated retry, dead-letter, and debug queues are included when present in the YAML.
                      Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
  
                      Examples:
                        sprmq purge --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
                        sprmq purge --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --allow-destructive --auto-approve
  destroy             Intentionally delete every virtual host declared in the YAML file.
                      Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
  
                      Examples:
                        sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
                        sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --allow-destructive --auto-approve
  export              Export broker topology to YAML.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq export --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --output-file topology.yaml
                        sprmq export --include-broker --output-file topology.yaml
  completion <shell>  Print shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, or pwsh.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq completion bash

commands.payload:
Description:
  RabbitMQ topology infrastructure-as-code for CI/CD and operator automation.
  
  Commands:
    validate  Validate topology files without contacting RabbitMQ.
    init      Create a topology YAML template.
    plan      Compare desired topology with the broker. Never changes broker state.
    apply     Apply only safe reconciliation operations. Refuses destructive changes.
    purge     Remove messages from all queues declared by the topology. Requires confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
    destroy   Intentionally delete declared virtual hosts. Requires confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
    export    Export broker topology to YAML.
    completion  Print shell completion scripts.
  
  Examples:
    sprmq init --template minimal --output-file topology.yaml
    sprmq plan --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml
    sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq purge --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --allow-destructive --auto-approve
    sprmq completion zsh

Usage:
  sprmq [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  init                Create a topology YAML file from a built-in template.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq init --template retry-dead-letter --output-file topology.yaml
  validate            Validate topology YAML syntax, normalization, and semantic consistency.
  
                      Example:

  plan                Compare desired topology against broker state and print a reconciliation plan.
                      This command never changes broker state.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq plan --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml
  apply               Apply a topology plan to RabbitMQ.
                      This command only performs safe reconciliation and refuses destructive plans unless --migrate is provided.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
                        sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --migrate
  purge               Remove all messages from every queue declared by the normalized topology.
                      Generated retry, dead-letter, and debug queues are included when present in the YAML.
                      Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.

                        sprmq purge --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --allow-destructive --auto-approve
  destroy             Intentionally delete every virtual host declared in the YAML file.
                      Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
  
                      Examples:

  export              Export broker topology to YAML.

                        sprmq export --include-broker --output-file topology.yaml
  completion <shell>  Print shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, or pwsh.
  
                      Example:
                        sprmq completion bash

result.stdout:
Description:
  Apply a topology plan to RabbitMQ.
  This command only performs safe reconciliation and refuses destructive plans unless --migrate is provided.
  
  Example:
    sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --migrate

Usage:
  sprmq apply [options]

Options:
  --file <file> (REQUIRED)           Path to the topology YAML file.
  --output <Json|Text>               Output format: text or json. [default: Text]
  --management-url <management-url>  RabbitMQ Management API URL.
  --username <username>              RabbitMQ username.
  --password <password>              RabbitMQ password.
  --vhost <vhost>                    Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.
  --dry-run                          Compute and print the apply result without changing the broker. [default: False]
  --migrate                          Allow supported exchange and queue migrations when immutable broker arguments changed. [default: False]
  --verbose                          Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Print shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, or pwsh.
  
  Example:
    sprmq completion bash

Usage:
  sprmq completion <shell> [options]

Arguments:
  <shell>  Target shell: bash, zsh, or pwsh.

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Intentionally delete every virtual host declared in the YAML file.
  Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.
  
  Examples:
    sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
    sprmq destroy --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --allow-destructive --auto-approve

Usage:
  sprmq destroy [options]

Options:
  --file <file> (REQUIRED)           Path to the topology YAML file.
  --output <Json|Text>               Output format: text or json. [default: Text]
  --management-url <management-url>  RabbitMQ Management API URL.
  --username <username>              RabbitMQ username.
  --password <password>              RabbitMQ password.
  --vhost <vhost>                    Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.
  --dry-run                          Compute and print the apply result without changing the broker. [default: False]
  --verbose                          Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]
  --allow-destructive                Allow destructive execution for commands that delete broker resources. [default: False]
  --auto-approve                     Skip the interactive confirmation prompt for destructive commands. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

  --auto-approve                     Skip the interactive confirmation prompt for destructive commands. [default: False]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Export broker topology to YAML.
  
  Example:
    sprmq export --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --output-file topology.yaml
    sprmq export --include-broker --output-file topology.yaml

Usage:
  sprmq export [options]

Options:
  --file <file>                      Optional topology YAML file used as a source for broker connection settings.
  --output <Json|Text>               Output format: text or json. [default: Text]
  --management-url <management-url>  RabbitMQ Management API URL.
  --username <username>              RabbitMQ username.
  --password <password>              RabbitMQ password.
  --vhost <vhost>                    Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.
  --output-file <output-file>        Output file path for export. Use '-' to write YAML to stdout. [default: -]
  --include-broker                   Include the resolved broker settings in the exported YAML. [default: False]
  --verbose                          Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

  --include-broker                   Include the resolved broker settings in the exported YAML. [default: False]
  --verbose                          Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a topology YAML file from a built-in template.
  
  Example:
    sprmq init --template retry-dead-letter --output-file topology.yaml

Usage:
  sprmq init [options]

Options:
  --template <template>        Template name. Available: debug, minimal, quorum, retry, retry-dead-letter, topic-routing. [default: minimal]
  --output-file <output-file>  Output file path for init. Use '-' to write YAML to stdout. [default: -]
  --force                      Overwrite the output file when it already exists. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --output-file <output-file>  Output file path for init. Use '-' to write YAML to stdout. [default: -]
  --force                      Overwrite the output file when it already exists. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information
